root_text.sn56.61:1.1: Atha kho bhagavā parittaṁ nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsuṁ āropetvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn56.61:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yo vāyaṁ mayā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito, ayaṁ vā mahāpathavī”ti?
root_text.sn56.61:1.4: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ, yadidaṁ—mahāpathavī; appamattakāyaṁ bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito.
root_text.sn56.61:1.5: Saṅkhampi na upeti, upanidhampi na upeti, kalabhāgampi na upeti mahāpathaviṁ upanidhāya bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito”ti.
root_text.sn56.61:2.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, appamattakā te sattā ye manussesu paccājāyanti; atha kho eteva bahutarā sattā ye aññatra manussehi paccājāyanti.
root_text.sn56.61:3.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.61:1.1: Then the Buddha, picking up a little bit of dirt on his fingernail, addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.61:1.3: Which is more: the little bit of dirt on my fingernail, or this great earth?”
translation_text.sn56.61:1.4: “Sir, the great earth is certainly more. The little bit of dirt on your fingernail is tiny.
translation_text.sn56.61:1.5: Compared to the great earth, it doesn’t count, there’s no comparison, it’s not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.61:2.1: “In the same way, the sentient beings reborn as humans are few, while those not reborn as humans are many.
translation_text.sn56.61:2.3: It’s because they haven’t seen the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.61:2.5: The noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.61:3.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
